remember: Students will be able to recall and describe the concept of government budget and its types.
Students will be able to identify and explain the different components of the budget.
Students will be able to recall the various methods of financing the budget deficit.
Students will be able to describe the impact of budget on the economy.
understand: Students will be able to understand the relationship between government budget and the economy.
Students will be able to explain how budget affects the level of economic activity.
Students will be able to understand the concept of fiscal policy and its role in stabilizing the economy.
Students will be able to explain the use of budget as an instrument of fiscal policy.
apply: Students will be able to apply the concepts of budget and fiscal policy to real-life scenarios.
Students will be able to analyze the budget and identify its implications on the economy.
Students will be able to evaluate the effectiveness of budget in achieving its objectives.
Students will be able to suggest possible changes in the budget to improve its impact on the economy.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the impact of budget on different sectors of the economy.
Students will be able to evaluate the relationship between budget and inflation.
Students will be able to analyze the impact of budget on employment and income distribution.
Students will be able to identify the limitations and challenges of using budget as a tool of fiscal policy.

ncert_objectives: Explain the concept of budget and its importance in the context of public finance.
Distinguish between revenue budget and capital budget.
Explain the different methods of financing budget deficit.
Discuss the impact of budget on the economy and the role of budget in stabilizing the economy.
board_exam_objectives: Explain the concept of budgetary deficit and its financing.
Discuss the impact of budget on employment and income distribution.
Explain the use of budget as an instrument of fiscal policy.
Analyze the budget and evaluate its effectiveness in achieving its objectives.
